 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.3","title":"TEST: Spotify Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"# TEST: Spotify Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)

## Goal
Mock-only tests for fcp.spotify across search, library, playlists, history, and playback control.

## Requirements
- No real Spotify calls in CI.
- Mock endpoints for:
  - search
  - saved tracks/albums/shows
  - playlist CRUD
  - recently played / top items
  - playback control endpoints
- Tests for:
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff
  - premium-required error cases
  - capability gating for playback and write operations
  - polling-backed player state stream behavior (bounded)

## Acceptance criteria
- Deterministic CI suite.
- Structured JSON logs with correlation_id + reason codes.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.4","title":"E2E: Spotify Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: Spotify Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Run the shared Mechanical Connector Compliance Runner against fcp.spotify.

## Must verify
- Default deny via CapabilityTokens.
- NetworkConstraints enforcement.
- Receipts/audit for write ops (playlist/library changes, playback control).
- Structured JSON logs with correlation_id + reason codes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Runner output bundle is green for fcp.spotify.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4xdb","title":"TEST-FUZZ-OBJECTID-VERIFIER: KeyedObjectIdVerifier verify path","description":"Add fuzz target for fcp-store::KeyedObjectIdVerifier::verify, the WAL-injection gate added by bead 4g0qr (object_id_verifier.rs:18-30). 4 hand-written unit tests cover the basic accept/reject classes; no fuzz coverage exists for arbitrary (header, body, claimed_id, key) inputs. Properties: (1) verify is panic-free; (2) genuine objects (claimed_id == derive_id(header, body, key)) accepted; (3) forged claimed_id rejected with ContentIdMismatch; (4) body bit-flip rejected; (5) wrong-key (different ObjectIdKey under same zone) rejected; (6) unknown-zone fails closed with VerifierKeyMissing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:16:02.653330Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:21:19.602472Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:21:19.602005Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","tests"],"comments":[{"id":2990,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-a4xdb","author":"jemanuel","text":"Added fuzz_object_id_verifier target.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a6ouw","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-e2e/spotify: handshake test masks connector handshake semantics","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/spotify_compliance_e2e.rs:89-120 serializes the real HandshakeRequest but discards the connector response (`let _raw = ...`) and fabricates a HandshakeResponse with fresh session_id, echoed nonce, and echoed requested capabilities. connectors/spotify/src/connector.rs:223-249 only returns protocol_version/connector_id/version/capabilities. This suite will stay green if the connector handshake stops surfacing required session semantics because the adapter invents them. Fix: assert directly on the connector handshake payload or make the connector emit a real HandshakeResponse-compatible shape and validate that end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:20:23.578854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:03:40.689350Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:03:40.688731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2844,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-a6ouw","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fix landed in commit c9561961 (by Jeff Emanuel, concurrent sibling commit; my equivalent edit matched the same fix pattern from terraform d18041eb). Adapter now serializes the real HandshakeRequest into handle_handshake, injects a generated session_id, and asserts on the connector-returned contract (protocol_version == 2.0, connector_id present, capabilities array present). capabilities_granted is filtered to only those the connector actually declares, matching production host semantics. Sibling commit also migrated host_allowed to delegate to fcp_sandbox::host_matches_allow_list for consistency with production canonicalization. Note: unable to run 'cargo test -p fcp-e2e --test spotify_compliance_e2e' locally because an unrelated fcp-sandbox build error (egress.rs:811 references canonicalize_host; should be canonicalize_hostname — out of scope for this bead) blocks the workspace build. The handshake code change is textually identical to the shipped fix.","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:03:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a7vd","title":"fcp.mongodb - MongoDB document database connector","description":"## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant MongoDB connector for document CRUD/aggregation and optional change-stream streaming with strict safety and secret handling.

## Credential handling (FCP2)
- Prefer secretless connectors: use `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` and let the egress proxy inject credentials at the network boundary when possible.
- If the protocol requires credential materialization inside the connector (e.g., some DB wire protocols), secrets MUST remain in memory only, be time-bounded, never touch disk, and never appear in logs/errors.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in the connector’s manifest/cap map bead if present).
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.
- For DB connectors, constrain raw TCP connects to explicit host:port (or Atlas endpoints) only; deny private ranges unless explicitly allowed by policy.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)

- `storage`
- `operational`
- `streaming`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Database (wire protocol / Atlas Data API) + (optional) change-stream streaming

## Summary

MongoDB connector for the Flywheel Connector Platform (FCP). This document database connector enables Rust applications to interact with MongoDB clusters and Atlas deployments through type-safe operations, aggregation pipelines, and comprehensive database management capabilities.

## Context

MongoDB is the leading document-oriented NoSQL database, widely used for flexible schema designs, horizontal scaling, and real-time analytics. This connector provides:

- **Document-Oriented Model**: Native BSON document support with flexible schemas
- **Aggregation Framework**: Powerful data processing pipelines
- **Atlas Integration**: Cloud-native MongoDB Atlas Data API support
- **Change Streams**: Real-time data change notifications
- **Sharding Support**: Distributed data across multiple shards

Value Score: 51/100 (Tier 4) - Moderate adoption in Rust ecosystem, strong use case for applications requiring flexible data models.

## Archetype

**Request-Response** - Standard synchronous operations for CRUD, aggregation, and administrative commands.

## Operations

### Read Zone
- `find` - Query documents with filters and projections
- `find_one` - Retrieve single document by query
- `aggregate` - Execute aggregation pipelines
- `count_documents` - Count matching documents
- `distinct` - Get distinct values for a field
- `list_collections` - List all collections in database
- `list_databases` - List all databases on server
- `list_indexes` - List indexes on a collection
- `watch` - Open change stream cursor (streaming context)

### Write Zone
- `insert_one` - Insert single document
- `insert_many` - Bulk insert documents
- `update_one` - Update single document
- `update_many` - Update multiple documents
- `replace_one` - Replace entire document
- `delete_one` - Delete single document
- `delete_many` - Delete multiple documents
- `find_one_and_update` - Atomically find and update
- `find_one_and_replace` - Atomically find and replace
- `find_one_and_delete` - Atomically find and delete
- `bulk_write` - Execute bulk operations

### Admin Zone
- `create_collection` - Create new collection with options
- `drop_collection` - Drop a collection
- `create_index` - Create index on collection
- `drop_index` - Remove index from collection
- `run_command` - Execute raw database command
- `create_database` - Implicitly create database
- `drop_database` - Drop entire database

## Design Notes

### Architecture

```
fcp-mongodb/
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs              # Public API exports
│   ├── client.rs           # MongoClient wrapper
│   ├── config.rs           # Configuration types
│   ├── connection.rs       # Connection pool management
│   ├── operations/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   ├── read.rs         # Find, aggregate operations
│   │   ├── write.rs        # Insert, update, delete ops
│   │   └── admin.rs        # Index, collection management
│   ├── atlas/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   └── data_api.rs     # Atlas Data API client
│   ├── pipeline/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   └── builder.rs      # Aggregation pipeline DSL
│   ├── types/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   ├── document.rs     # BSON document helpers
│   │   ├── filter.rs       # Query filter builders
│   │   └── options.rs      # Operation options
│   └── error.rs            # Error types
├── Cargo.toml
└── README.md
```

### Connection Configuration

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::{MongoConfig, MongoClient};

let config = MongoConfig::builder()
    .endpoint("mongodb+srv://cluster.mongodb.net/") // no credentials in URI
    .auth_credential_id("cred:<mongodb_auth>")      // secret reference; never stored on disk
    .database("myapp")
    .app_name("flywheel-connector")
    .min_pool_size(5)
    .max_pool_size(100)
    .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10))
    .server_selection_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))
    .retry_writes(true)
    .retry_reads(true)
    .build()?;

let client = MongoClient::connect(config).await?;
```

### Document Operations

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::{doc, Document};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct User {
    #[serde(rename = "_id")]
    id: ObjectId,
    name: String,
    email: String,
    profile: UserProfile,
    tags: Vec<String>,
    created_at: DateTime,
}

// Type-safe operations
let users = client.collection::<User>("users");

// Insert
let user = User { /* ... */ };
let result = users.insert_one(&user).await?;

// Query with filter
let filter = doc! { "email": "user@example.com" };
let user = users.find_one(filter).await?;

// Update with operators
let update = doc! {
    "$set": { "name": "New Name" },
    "$push": { "tags": "verified" }
};
users.update_one(filter, update).await?;
```

### Aggregation Pipeline

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::pipeline::{Pipeline, stage};

let pipeline = Pipeline::new()
    .match_stage(doc! { "status": "active" })
    .lookup(stage::Lookup {
        from: "orders",
        local_field: "user_id",
        foreign_field: "customer_id",
        as_field: "orders",
    })
    .unwind("$orders")
    .group(stage::Group {
        id: "$user_id",
        fields: vec![
            ("total_orders", stage::Accumulator::Sum(1)),
            ("total_spent", stage::Accumulator::Sum("$orders.amount")),
        ],
    })
    .sort(doc! { "total_spent": -1 })
    .limit(10)
    .build();

let results = users.aggregate::<UserStats>(pipeline).await?;
```

### Index Management

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::index::{IndexModel, IndexOptions};

// Create single field index
let index = IndexModel::new(doc! { "email": 1 })
    .options(IndexOptions::builder()
        .unique(true)
        .name("email_unique")
        .build());
users.create_index(index).await?;

// Create compound index
let compound = IndexModel::new(doc! { 
    "status": 1, 
    "created_at": -1 
});
users.create_index(compound).await?;

// Create text index for search
let text_index = IndexModel::new(doc! { 
    "name": "text", 
    "description": "text" 
});
users.create_index(text_index).await?;

// Create TTL index
let ttl_index = IndexModel::new(doc! { "expires_at": 1 })
    .options(IndexOptions::builder()
        .expire_after(Duration::from_secs(0))
        .build());
sessions.create_index(ttl_index).await?;
```

### Atlas Data API Support

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::atlas::{AtlasClient, AtlasConfig};

let config = AtlasConfig::builder()
    .base_url("https://data.mongodb-api.com/app/data-xxxxx/endpoint/data/v1")
    .credential_id("cred:<atlas_data_api_key>") // secret reference; never stored on disk
    .data_source("Cluster0")
    .database("myapp")
    .build()?;

let atlas = AtlasClient::new(config);

// Operations work the same way
let users = atlas.collection::<User>("users");
let result = users.find(filter).await?;
```

### Change Streams

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::change_stream::{ChangeStream, ChangeEvent};

let mut stream = users.watch()
    .full_document(FullDocument::UpdateLookup)
    .pipeline(vec![
        doc! { "$match": { "operationType": "insert" } }
    ])
    .start().await?;

while let Some(event) = stream.next().await {
    match event?.operation_type {
        OperationType::Insert => {
            let doc = event.full_document.unwrap();
            println!("New user: {:?}", doc);
        }
        OperationType::Update => { /* ... */ }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

### Transaction Support

```rust
let mut session = client.start_session().await?;

session.start_transaction().await?;

// Operations within transaction
let users = client.collection::<User>("users");
users.insert_one(&user).with_session(&mut session).await?;

let orders = client.collection::<Order>("orders");
orders.insert_one(&order).with_session(&mut session).await?;

session.commit_transaction().await?;
```

### Rate Limiting Integration

```rust
use fcp_ratelimit::{RateLimiter, TokenBucket};

let limiter = RateLimiter::new(TokenBucket::new(
    100,  // requests per second
    Duration::from_secs(1),
));

let client = MongoClient::builder()
    .config(config)
    .rate_limiter(limiter)
    .build().await?;
```

### Telemetry Integration

```rust
use fcp_telemetry::{Telemetry, SpanKind};

let client = MongoClient::builder()
    .config(config)
    .telemetry(Telemetry::new("fcp-mongodb"))
    .build().await?;

// Operations automatically traced
// Span: mongodb.find { db.name: "myapp", db.collection: "users", ... }
```

## Dependencies

- `fcp-core` - Core FCP traits and types
- `fcp-ratelimit` - Rate limiting for connection management
- `fcp-telemetry` - Observability and tracing
- `mongodb` - Official MongoDB Rust driver (v2.8+)
- `bson` - BSON serialization
- `serde` - Serialization framework
- `tokio` - Async runtime

## Implementation Notes

1. **Driver Choice**: Use the official `mongodb` crate as the foundation, wrapping it with FCP patterns
2. **Connection Pooling**: Leverage the driver's built-in connection pool with configurable sizes
3. **Atlas Data API**: Provide alternative HTTP-based access for serverless/edge deployments
4. **Schema Validation**: Optional JSON Schema validation on collections
5. **Retryable Operations**: Enable retryable reads/writes by default for resilience
6. **Compression**: Support zstd/snappy/zlib compression for network efficiency

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Connect to standalone, replica set, and sharded clusters
- [ ] All CRUD operations implemented with type-safe wrappers
- [ ] Aggregation pipeline builder with compile-time validation
- [ ] Index management (create, drop, list)
- [ ] Change streams for real-time updates
- [ ] Transaction support for multi-document ACID
- [ ] Atlas Data API client for serverless scenarios
- [ ] Connection pool metrics exposed via telemetry
- [ ] Rate limiting integration for throttling
- [ ] Comprehensive error types mapping driver errors
- [ ] Integration tests against MongoDB test containers
- [ ] Documentation with examples for all operations

## References

- [MongoDB Rust Driver](https://docs.rs/mongodb/latest/mongodb/)
- [MongoDB Atlas Data API](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/api/data-api/)
- [BSON Specification](https://bsonspec.org/)
- [MongoDB Aggregation](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/aggregation/)

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a7vd.1","title":"fcp.mongodb: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"## Goal
Produce the full FCP2 manifest and capability map for this connector.

This bead is where we translate the connector’s functional surface (operations) into a **mechanically checkable** contract:
- operation schemas + schema IDs
- capability families per operation
- per-operation NetworkConstraints (default deny)
- sandbox profile requirements
- declared state model (stateless / singleton_writer / crdt)

## Required content (must be explicit)
- Operation list is exhaustive and stable for the current phase.
- Capability IDs DO NOT encode hostnames/ports.
- NetworkConstraints are minimal and per-operation.
- Risk tier for each operation (Safe / Dangerous).
- Explicit bounds: timeouts, max response sizes, pagination limits.

## Tests
- Add good/bad manifest examples for the compliance runner.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes static checks in `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`.
- Default-deny is enforced by manifest + capability requirements.

Hash: 774e98368d9f1013d860cbdf2e902c2a54b8f8db5badb3da0d4f777fbb551879. Test vectors: manifest_mongodb_good.toml + manifest_mongodb_bad.toml (3 violations: NORMATIVE dup cap, zone conflict, timeout=0).","created_at":"2026-03-02T03:22:28Z"}]}
